title: What are we doing here?
body: Nine days into open strikes between the US and Iran, the story the establishment press keeps burying is the one about us. The Intercept reports the Pentagon's cover-up is unraveling: after insisting American forces were fine, it now concedes nearly 100 troops were wounded in Iran's strikes, and its own sources say the real number is higher. When the count of your own casualties becomes something to manage instead of disclose, that tells you who this war is being run for.

The escalation is not slowing. Dropsite News has the two sides trading fire for a ninth straight day, attacks now in the Strait of Hormuz, and a House vote to fuse parts of the US and Israeli militaries. Al Jazeera puts the cost so far, from Defense Secretary Hegseth's own mouth, at $37.5 billion. Iran is holding funerals for children killed in a school strike.
So ask the question Washington won't: what are we doing here, and who decided it for us? The independent outlets are tallying the cost. The official channels are counting on you not to look.
